data.title: China Merchants Shekou Industrial Zone Logs 30 Billion Yuan in Contracted Sales in December 2024
data.description:
China Merchants Shekou Industrial Zone Holdings recorded a contracted sales amount of 30.2 billion yuan in December 2024 after selling 1.2 million square meters of contracted sales area, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

For the entire 2024, the Chinese real estate developer generated a contracted sales amount of 219.3 billion yuan after selling 9.4 million square meters.

Moreover, the company acquired eight land plots in November 2024 for 10.1 billion yuan.

data.title: Ichigo Office REIT Investment's Occupancy Rate Steady in December
data.description:
Ichigo Office REIT Investment reported an occupancy rate of 95.5% for December, unchanged from November, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on Thursday.

The real estate investment trust's occupancy rate slipped to 97.2% in the Tokyo metropolitan but increased to 94.1% in central Tokyo, the filing said.

Occupancy fell to 94.5% in four major regional cities and was flat at 99.1% in other regional cities.

The REIT's portfolio consists of 91 assets with a leased area of 265,000 square meters, the filing said.

data.title: Templeton Emerging Markets Investment Trust Purchases for Cancellation Over 170,000 Ordinary Shares
data.description:
Templeton Emerging Markets Investment Trust purchased for cancellation 170,447 ordinary shares at 1.65 pounds sterling apiece, according to a Friday filing with the New Zealand bourse.

The company's share capital now consists of nearly 1.05 billion shares in issue and 103.8 million shares in treasury, the filing said.

data.title: SATS Secures Cargo, Ground Handling Contracts from Air India
data.description:
SATS and wholly owned subsidiary, World Flight Services, secured cargo and ground handling contracts from Air India, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The airline renewed 11 contracts and awarded 14 new contracts through a global tendering process, furthering its partnership with the company and its subsidiary.

Through the new contracts, the company will handle new cargo and ground stations across North America, UK, Europe and Asia.

data.title: India's Sensex Opens 0.1% Higher at 77682.59, Led by Tech Stocks
Japan's economy minister vows to coordinate policy with BOJ
data.description:
Key points:
  • Calling end to deflation requires caution, Akazawa says
  • Japan must shift to growth driven by higher wages, investment
  • Japan can drop crisis-mode tools once end to deflation called
  • Govt call on deflation may affect BOJ's rate-hike timing

Japan's government will closely coordinate policy with the central bank as the economy is at a critical stage in shifting to growth driven by higher wages and investment, economy minister Ryosei Akazawa said on Friday.

Japan has long been stuck in a "deflationary, vicious cycle" where prices and wages have stagnated, causing households to curb spending and companies to focus on cost cutting, he said.

"It's very important to dispel this deflationary mindset, and achieve growth driven by higher wages and investment," Akazawa told a regular news conference, stressing the need to prod firms to raise wages at a pace exceeding inflation.

"There's no change in the government's stance to closely coordinate with the Bank of Japan, and nimbly guide policy in accordance with economic and price developments," he said.

The remarks come ahead of the BOJ's policy-setting meeting on Jan. 23-24, when some analysts expect the central bank to raise interest rates from the current 0.25%.

Akazawa is in charge of deciding whether the government can declare that Japan is no longer in deflation. When doing so, the government looks not just at underlying price moves but also at whether the economy has completely eradicated the risk of slipping back into deflation.

The decision could affect the timing of future BOJ rate hikes, by underscoring the government's confidence that the economy no longer needs crisis-mode stimulus measures, some analysts said.

Akazawa said the decision on whether to declare a complete exit from deflation must be made "cautiously" by scrutinising the factors behind Japan's underlying inflation.

"Once we are able to fully declare an end to deflation, we can relieve ourselves from deploying tools we had in store to fight deflation," he said.

Japan's economy expanded an annualised 1.2% in the three months to September, slowing from the previous quarter's 2.2% increase, with consumption up a feeble 0.7% as rising living costs hurt households.

Rising food and fuel costs, coupled with higher labour costs driven by a tight job market, have kept inflation above the BOJ's 2% target for nearly three years. Core inflation hit 2.7% in November, keeping the BOJ under pressure to hike rates.

Despite rising inflation, the government has held off on declaring an end to "deflation", holding the view that Japan's economy still faces risks of another downturn.

Calling a formal end to deflation would draw a line under two decades of falling prices and economic stagnation that followed the collapse of Japan's asset-inflated bubble economy that stretched from 1986 to 1991.

Data released on Friday showed household spending fell at a slower pace than expected in November, which followed a survey showing base salary rising that month at the fastest annual pace in 1992.

The BOJ said on Thursday that wage hikes were broadening due to structural labour shortages, suggesting that conditions for a near-term rate hike were continuing to fall into place.

data.title: Fast Retailing's Fiscal Q1 Profit Jumps 22%; Tokyo Shares Dip 7%
data.description:
Fast Retailing's attributable profit rose 22% to 132 billion yen in the fiscal first quarter from 107.8 billion yen in the year-ago period, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong stock exchange.

Earnings per share at the fast fashion company increased to 429.51 yen from 350.88 yen in the previous year.

Revenue grew 10% to 895.2 billion yen from 810.8 billion yen a year earlier.

The retail company expects fiscal year 2025 attributable profit to rise 3.5% to 385.5 billion yen and EPS is seen to reach 1,255.14 yen for the entire year. Revenue is forecast to grow 9.5% to 3.400 trillion yen.

Shares fell 7% in Tokyo and 4% in Hong Kong during Friday's morning trading.

data.title: Mithril Resources Brings in Consultants for Mexico Silver, Gold Project
data.description:
Mithril Resources brought Daniel Kunz and Associates on board to support work at its Copalquin silver-gold property in Mexico, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company engaged the consulting firm for a period of 12 months, the filing said. The services the consultants will provide include review and analysis of all geological parameters derived from core logging, multi-element assays, and ongoing support for resource estimation work.

The consultants will be issued 735,000 unlisted share options exercisable at AU$0.70 for two years from the date of issue.

The consulting firm is founded and led by Daniel Kunz, formerly the founding chief executive of Prime Mining, which is working on its Los Reyes precious metals project, within the same geologic region as Mithril's Copalquin project.

data.title: Onward Holdings' Attributable Profit Marginally Up in Fiscal Nine Months; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Onward Holdings' profit attributable to owners of the parent gained 0.6% to 5.78 billion yen in the fiscal nine months ended Nov. 30, 2024, from 5.75 billion yen a year ago.

The apparel manufacturer said earnings per share rose to 42.54 yen from 42.29 yen a year ago, according to a Thursday filing.

Net sales gained 6.1% to 149.9 billion yen from 141.4 billion yen a year earlier.

In a separate filing, the company said it revised its financial forecast for fiscal 2025 after an assessment of performance and recognition of deferred tax assets, expecting profits to surpass earlier forecasts.

It forecasted attributable profit to grow 28.6% to 8.5 billion yen or 62.62 yen per share and net sales to gain 10.7% to 210 billion yen for the fiscal year ending Feb. 28, 2025.

The company also updated its dividend forecast, raising the year-end dividend to 26 yen per share, up from the previous forecast of 24 yen.

Onward Holdings' shares shed nearly 3% recently.

data.title: India's TCS jumps 4% in pre-open trade on signs of demand revival
data.description:
Shares of India's top software services exporter Tata Consultancy Services jumped 4% in pre-open trade on Friday, as investors cheered its assessment that there are early signs of a demand revival.

TCS CEO K Krithivasan told reporters in a post-earnings press conference on Thursday that the company was seeing more confidence in discretionary programmes in the coming years.

Kritivasan's comments overshadowed a weak performance in North American revenue for the fifth straight quarter.

data.title: DEADLINE REMINDER: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Investigates Claims on Behalf of Investors of AstraZeneca
data.description:
Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Securities Litigation Partner James (Josh) Wilson Encourages Investors Who Suffered Losses Exceeding $100,000 In AstraZenecaTo Contact Him Directly To Discuss Their Options

If you suffered losses exceeding $100,000 in AstraZeneca between between February 23, 2022 and December 17, 2024 and would like to discuss your legal rights, call Faruqi & Faruqi partner Josh Wilson directly at 877-247-4292 or 212-983-9330 (Ext. 1310).

[You may also click here for additional information]

New York, New York--(Newsfile Corp. - January 9, 2025) - Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP, a leading national securities law firm, is investigating potential claims against AstraZeneca PLC ("AstraZeneca" or the "Company") and reminds investors of the Feb. 21, 2025 deadline to seek the role of lead plaintiff in a federal securities class action that has been filed against the Company.

Faruqi & Faruqi is a leading national securities law firm with offices in New York, Pennsylvania, California and Georgia. The firm has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors since its founding in 1995. See www.faruqilaw.com.

As detailed below, the complaint alleges that the Company and its executives violated federal securities laws by making false and/or misleading statements and/or failing to disclose that (1) AstraZeneca engaged in insurance fraud in China; (2) as a result, AstraZeneca faced heightened legal exposure in China, which eventually resulted in the AstraZeneca China President being detained by Chinese law enforcement authorities; (3) as a result, AstraZeneca understated its legal risks; (4) the foregoing, once revealed, could materially harm AstraZeneca's business activities in China; and (5) as a result, Defendants' statements about its business, operations, and prospects, were materially false and misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis at all relevant times.

On October 30, 2024, AstraZeneca announced that Leon Wang, Executive Vice President International and AstraZeneca China President, was "cooperating with an ongoing investigation by Chinese authorities."

On this news, AstraZeneca American Depositary Shares ("ADS") fell 3.1% on October 30, 2024.

Then, on November 5, 2024, Yicai Global published an article entitled "AstraZeneca Insurance Fraud Involves Dozens of Senior Executives in China, Source Says." This article stated that "[d]ozens of senior executives at AstraZeneca China have been implicated in an ongoing insurance fraud case as of last week, according to a person familiar with the matter." Further, it stated that "[o]ver the past three years, insurance fraud cases involving AstraZeneca have surfaced in Shenzhen as well as the provinces of Fujian and Jiangxi. [. . .] These cases amount to the largest insurance fraud in the nation's pharmaceutical sector for years, a person familiar with the matter pointed out."

On this news, AstraZeneca ADSs fell a further 7.2% on November 5, 2024.

The court-appointed lead plaintiff is the investor with the largest financial interest in the relief sought by the class who is adequate and typical of class members who directs and oversees the litigation on behalf of the putative class. Any member of the putative class may move the Court to serve as lead plaintiff through counsel of their choice, or may choose to do nothing and remain an absent class member. Your ability to share in any recovery is not affected by the decision to serve as a lead plaintiff or not.

Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP also encourages anyone with information regarding AstraZeneca's conduct to contact the firm, including whistleblowers, former employees, shareholders and others.

To learn more about the AstraZeneca class action, go to www.faruqilaw.com/AZN or call Faruqi & Faruqi partner Josh Wilson directly at 877-247-4292 or 212-983-9330 (Ext. 1310).

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn, on X, or on Facebook.

Attorney Advertising. The law firm responsible for this advertisement is Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP (www.faruqilaw.com). Prior results do not guarantee or predict a similar outcome with respect to any future matter. We welcome the opportunity to discuss your particular case. All communications will be treated in a confidential manner.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/236494

data.title: China's December exports are expected to jump from November - beat the tariffs race
data.description:
China's December 2024 trade data is scheduled for release on January 12, 2025, at 10:00 AM China Standard Time:

  • 0300 GMT
  • 2200 US Eastern time on January 11

China's exports are expected to have expanded at a faster pace in December than in November, with firms hurrying to move inventory to major markets ahead of Trump's return to the White House this month and the accompanying fresh trade risks.

Imports are expected to have fallen, for the third month in a row.

data.title: Singapore Dollar Steady Ahead of U.S. Non-Farm Payrolls Report — Market Talk
data.description: The Singapore dollar is trading steadily against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian session ahead of the U.S. non-farm payrolls report due out later today. The report is expected to show job additions slowed to 165,000 in December from 227,000 in November, as effects of one-off weather impact diminish, Maybank analysts say in a FX Research & Strategy report. Figures in line with estimates may keep USD Index around elevated levels, the analysts say. However, readings quite above or below expectations could lead to significant movements for the USD Index in either direction, the analysts add. USD/SGD is little changed at 1.3685. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Market Chatter: CJ Foodville's Bakery Franchise to Open First Outlet in Malaysia in H1
data.description:
South Korean food and entertainment giant CJ Group's subsidiary CJ Foodville's bakery chain Tous les Jours will open its first Malaysian outlet in the first half of 2025, The Korea Herald reported Thursday.

The company partnered with local retailer Stream Empire under a master franchise agreement, which allows expansion and potential sub-franchising in the region, the report said.

CJ Corporation is the holding company of CJ Group.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Market Chatter: Chinese Commercial Banks Trim Consumer Loan Rates Before Lunar New Year Holidays
data.description:
China's commercial banks are offering attractive interest rates on consumer loans to boost lending ahead of the Lunar New Year, YiCai Global reported Thursday.

China Minsheng Bank and Bank of Beijing have dropped their preferential interest rates to 2.76% for loans of up to 300,000 yuan and 1 million yuan, respectively, for VIP clients and mortgage holders, according to the report.

Industry insiders said the pre-holiday period is a peak time for consumer loans as banks move to enhance customer loyalty and expand lending volumes early in the year, YiCai Global wrote.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: China's Zijin Mining in talks to buy controlling stake in Zangge Mining
data.description:
China's Zijin Mining said on Friday it was in talks with Zangge Mining's top two shareholders to buy a potential controlling stake in the company.

Zijin is in talks with Tibet Zangge Venture Capital and Ningbo Meishan Bonded Port Area Xinsha Hongyun Investment Management, which collectively own about 40% stake in the 46.63 billion yuan ($6.36 billion) miner, according to LSEG data.

The potential deal would allow Zijin to consolidate its ownership over the Julong copper project in Tibet, its joint venture with Zangge.

Last year, Zijin had won government approval to expand the Julong mine's output to about 350,000 metric tons per day, making it the largest single copper mine in China.

The talks further underline Zijin's attempts to cash in on growing demand for battery metals, as it prepares to start production at its first lithium exploration project in the Democratic Republic of Congo next year.

($1 = 7.3319 Chinese yuan)

data.title: New Property Listings in New Zealand See Weakest December on Record
data.description:
New property listings in New Zealand declined to the lowest level on record, while the average asking price also fell back to its level in April 2021, according to a Thursday report by realestate.co.nz.

There were 4,767 properties listed in December 2024, down by more 57% from November and by 1.3% compared with the year-ago period. This was the lowest December listings on record since the property website's tracking started.

While December has historically been a muted month, the end of 2024 was "record-breakingly quiet," said Vanessa Williams, spokesperson for realestate.co.nz. Nine of 19 regions also reached their lowest-ever December levels, per the report.

Meanwhile, the average asking price fell 4.2% year on year to NZ$842,476, the lowest since April 2021.

data.title: Metals edge up, Trump tariff uncertainties in focus
data.description:
Most base metal prices edged up on Friday, although uncertainties about U.S. President-elect Donald Trump's tariff plans and a strong U.S. dollar kept a lid on gains.

Three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange (LME) had edged up 0.5% to $9,120 per metric ton by 0237 GMT.

The dollar index was at $109.07, slightly lower than the two-year-high hit on Jan. 2 but poised to extend its longest run of weekly gains in more than a year, due to rising bond yields and the anticipation of another robust U.S. jobs report.

A stronger dollar makes it more expensive for holders of other currencies to buy greenback-priced commodities.

"Copper still faces downward pressure if the dollar gains strength when Trump decides to change some tariffs," Jinrui Futures said in a note.

Financial markets fear Trump's trade tariffs will boost inflation. That has helped the dollar remain strong, underpinned by rising Treasury yields.

Federal Reserve Bank of Boston President Susan Collins, on Thursday, advocated a patient and gradual approach to U.S. interest rate cuts due to significant uncertainty.

Earlier this week, reports circulated that Trump's team was contemplating selective tariffs on sectors crucial to national or economic security. However, Trump denied these reports.

The most-traded February copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) added 0.8% to 75,310 yuan ($10,271.83) a ton.

LME aluminium (CMAl3) rose 0.6% to $2,554 a ton, nickel gained 0.3% to $15,535, zinc added 0.4% to $2,860, tin advanced 0.8% to $30,100, while lead rose 1.0% to $1,947.

SHFE aluminium rose 1.7% to 20,115 yuan a ton, nickel gained 0.8% to 125,870 yuan, zinc rose 0.6% to 24,115 yuan, lead lost 0.1% to 16,555 yuan, and tin rose 0.1% to 252,290 yuan.

For the top stories in metals and other news, click

or

($1 = 7.3317 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: Zhejiang Crystal-Optech Expects Net Profit to Jump Up to 87% in December 2024; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Zhejiang Crystal-Optech expects to record a net profit attributable to shareholders of between 1 billion yuan and 1.12 billion yuan in 2024, jumping up to 87% from 600.2 million yuan a year earlier, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

Earnings per share will be between 0.72 yuan and 0.81 yuan, compared to 0.43 yuan in the year-ago period, the Chinese optics and optoelectronics manufacturer said.

The company's shares dropped less than 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Cyprus Trade Deficit Widens Sharply in November
data.description:
The trade deficit in Cyprus widened sharply to EUR 752.2 million in November 2024 from EUR 333 million in the corresponding month of the previous year, preliminary estimates showed.

Imports rose 2.7% year-on-year to EUR 1,128.7 million in November, as arrivals from the third countries surged (10.4%), although it dropped from EU countries (-2.8%).

Meanwhile, exports slumped by 49.5% to EUR 376.5 million, as sales dropped significantly to both EU (-8.3%) and third (-54.8%) countries.

Considering January to November period, the country's trade deficit narrowed to EUR 7,139.6 million from EUR 7,908.7 million in the same period a year earlier.

data.title: F&C Investment Trust Buys Back 15,000 Ordinary Shares
data.description:
F&C Investment Trust bought back 15,000 ordinary shares at an average price of 11.40 pounds sterling apiece, according to a Thursday filing with the New Zealand bourse.

The investment trust now holds more than 79.6 million ordinary shares in treasury and has almost 482.2 million ordinary shares in issue, the filing said.

data.title: Polyplex Board Approves $65 Million Investment for New Manufacturing Unit in India
data.description:
Polyplex Corporation has received board approval to set up a biaxially oriented polyethylene terephthalate (BOPET) manufacturing film plant in India with a proposed investment of $65 million, according to a Thursday filing to the stock exchanges.

The company plans to add a capacity of 52,400 MTPA for BOPET film to its existing capacity of 90,000 MTPA for both BOPET and biaxially oriented polypropylene (BOPP) films.

The capacity addition is expected to be completed in the first half of the financial year 2027-28.

data.title: Gold prices on track for weekly gain; US data on tap
data.description:
Key points:
  • US non-farm payrolls report due at 8:30 a.m. ET
  • Good support for gold at $2,650, analyst says
  • Silver, platinum, and palladium poised for weekly gains

Gold prices hovered near a four-week high on Friday, poised for their best week since mid-November, as investors awaited U.S. jobs data to gauge how aggressively the Federal Reserve might cut interest rates this year.

Spot gold edged 0.1% higher to $2,672.64 per ounce, as of 0309 GMT. Bullion has gained more than 1% so far this week.

U.S. gold futures rose 0.2% to $2,696.30.

Investors will closely watch the key government payrolls report scheduled for release at 8:30 a.m. ET. According to a Reuters survey, non-farm payrolls are expected to have increased by 160,000 in December, following a jump of 227,000 in November.

"We expect gold to drop a little in case the Non-farm payroll (NFP) report comes on a higher side. Reports suggest that President-elect Trump may announce an economic emergency to roll out tariffs smoothly. All these should support the dollar and gold might just decline in the near term. Having said that, $2,650 is a good support," said Jigar Trivedi, senior analyst at Reliance Securities.

Gold prices strengthened to a nearly four-week high in the previous session, supported by safe-haven demand, while investors weighed how U.S. President-elect Trump's policies would impact the economy and inflation.

Trump will return to office on Jan. 20 and his proposed tariffs and protectionist policies are expected to fuel inflation.

Kansas City Federal Reserve President Jeff Schmid signalled on Thursday a reluctance to cut interest rates again as the U.S. central bank comes into the New Year facing a resilient economy and inflation that remains above its 2% target.

Gold is used as a hedge against inflation, although higher interest rates reduce the appeal of holding the non-yielding asset.

Spot silver was up 0.4% to $30.24 per ounce, platinum dropped 0.1% to $957.43 and palladium added 1.4% to $939.13. All three metals were headed for weekly gains.

data.title: DEADLINE REMINDER: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Investigates Claims on Behalf of Investors of Kyverna Therapeutics

data.title: Flagship Minerals Completes Name, Ticker Change
data.description:
Flagship Minerals completed its rebranding from Pan Asia Metals, with the exploration company now trading under the ticker code "FLG," according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Shareholders approved the change on Dec. 31, 2024, indicating a shift from the company's exclusive focus on Asia and on lithium, the filing said.

data.title: Guangdong Haid's Profit Forecast to Jump Up to 75% in 2024; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Guangdong Haid Group expects its net profit attributable to shareholders to rise to 4.25 billion yuan to 4.80 billion yuan in 2024, jumping up to 75% from the previous year, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

The Chinese agricultural and animal husbandry company's attributable profit a year ago was at 2.74 billion yuan.

The company attributed the rise to a 9% year on year increase in feed sales and a further expansion in its market share.

Earnings per share will be between 2.55 yuan and 2.89 yuan, compared to 1.66 yuan in the prior year, the disclosure said.

The company's shares jumped 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Financial Times - Jan 10
data.description:
The following are the top stories in the Financial Times. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

Headline

- Nathaniel Rothschild files lawsuit against Lars Windhorst

- General Atlantic-backed biotech start-up secures $410mn in initial funding round

- BlackRock quits climate change group in latest green climbdown

- UK government seeks to soothe bond market nerves after borrowing costs soar

Overview

- British financier Nathaniel Rothschild has filed a lawsuit against Lars Windhorst in London's High Court, within six months of the banking dynasty scion agreeing to become chair of the German entrepreneur's investment firm.

- UK-based Verdiva Bio has raised over $410 million in one of the largest initial funding rounds in the biotech sector, aiming to develop drugs discovered in China to compete with Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk in the growing obesity market.

- BlackRock has exited the global voluntary climate change group Net Zero Asset Managers, in the wake of Donald Trump's election as US president and heightened regulatory scrutiny.

- The British government pledged to adhere to its fiscal rules on Thursday in an effort to calm turmoil in UK bond markets, as borrowing costs surged to their highest levels since the financial crisis.

data.title: Indonesia Retail Sales Rise the Least in 10 Months
data.description:
Retail sales in Indonesia grew by 0.9% year-on-year in November 2024, slowing from a 1.5% rise in the previous month.

This marked the seventh consecutive month of expansion in retail turnover but the weakest growth since January, due to a notable slowdown in food sales (2.5% vs 3.3% in October), fuels (8.8% vs 9.3%), clothing (0.6% vs 1.7%), and automotive parts & accessories (7.2% vs 8.8%).

Additionally, trade further declined in information & communication (-23.1% vs -25.1%), cultural & recreational goods (-2.4% vs -0.8%), and home appliances (-7.2% vs -6.5%).

Retail sales are expected to increase by 1.0% in December.

On a monthly basis, retail sales fell by 0.4%, following a flat reading in October.

data.title: Yen Holds Decline on BOJ Rate Hike Uncertainty
data.description:
The Japanese yen traded around 158.2 per dollar on Friday, staying near multi-month lows as uncertainty persists over the timing of interest rate hikes by the Bank of Japan.

Japan’s Economy Minister Ryosei Akazawa stated that the economy is at a “critical stage” in overcoming the public’s deflationary mindset but provided no clear indication of when the BOJ may raise rates.

On the data front, household spending in Japan declined by 0.4% year-on-year in November, while household income rose 0.7%.

Externally, the yen faced additional pressure from a recent widening of the US-Japan yield differential, driven by hawkish signals from the US Federal Reserve.

Moreover, investors are awaiting the release of the US jobs report on Friday, which could strengthen expectations for fewer Fed rate cuts this year.

data.title: Waaree Renewable Technologies Forms New Non-Profit Organization
data.description:
Waaree Renewable Technologies has incorporated a new non-profit organization named Waaree India Foundation, according to a Thursday BSE filing.

The company will contribute up to 20,000 Indian rupees as a guarantee amount for the new entity, which will promote healthcare, education, welfare, shelter, and other activities, the filing said.
